title: French architects of Iran nuclear deal watch Trump unravel their work
subhead: French diplomats who helped negotiate the 2015 Vienna agreement, aimed to prevent Tehran from acquiring nuclear weapons, now watch bitterly as decisions by the Trump administration unravel their work.

title: Israel PM says targeted a top Hezbollah commander in Beirut strike
Gaza police become targets for Israeli army
Court overturns dismissal of former Rwandan first lady's 'complicity in genocide' charges
subhead: The Paris appeals court ruled that the investigation into Agathe Habyarimana must resume. She is accused of acts that constitute 'complicity in genocide and crimes against humanity' during the Rwandan genocide.

title: Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
subhead: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

title: PSG-Bayern Munich: What makes a match legendary?
subhead: The celebrations surrounding the spectacular Champions League match between Paris Saint-Germain and Bayern Munich reveal the growing dominance of entertainment over competition, writes journalist Jérôme Latta in his column.

title: Ted Turner, founder of CNN, is dead
subhead: The four-time 'Yachtsman of the Year' relished a challenge. When he launched Cable News Network in Atlanta in 1980, few believed the all-news channel would succeed. Also a philanthropist and founder of the United Nations Foundation, Turner died at the age of 87.

Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
subhead: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

title: Europeans rethink collective defense amid Trump's threats against NATO
subhead: Would the US intervene in the event of an attack on Europe? European leaders, who met Monday in Yerevan alongside Canada, are growing increasingly doubtful after Trump announced the withdrawal of 5,000 American troops from Germany.

title: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
subhead: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

title: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
A European tax on oil superprofits is necessary
